def_en: guidance or recommendations about what to do (good advice, expert advice, piece of advice)
def_zh: 建议（好建议，专家建议，一条建议）
quote_en: My uncle gave me sound advice about saving money.
quote_zh: 我叔叔给了我很稳妥的存钱建议。
ex_en: In ancient Greece, people traveled to Delphi to seek advice from the famous oracle.
ex_zh: 在古希腊，人们前往德尔斐寻求著名神谕的建议。

def_en: the feeling of being slightly angry or bothered (great annoyance, constant annoyance, cause annoyance)
def_zh: 烦恼（极大的烦恼，不断的烦扰，引起烦恼）
quote_en: The dripping tap was a minor annoyance that kept me awake.
quote_zh: 滴水的水龙头是个小烦恼，让我睡不着。
ex_en: During the American Revolution, British soldiers considered the swarms of mosquitoes in the swamps a major annoyance.
ex_zh: 美国独立战争期间，英国士兵认为沼泽地里成群的蚊子是一大烦扰。

def_en: the act of being present at a place (regular attendance, perfect attendance, attendance record)
def_zh: 出席（定期出席，全勤，出勤记录）
quote_en: Your attendance at practice will affect whether you play in the game.
quote_zh: 你训练的出勤会影响你是否能上场参赛。
ex_en: In medieval universities, attendance was taken by calling each student's name from a handwritten roll.
ex_zh: 在中世纪的大学里，通过从手写名册上点名来记录出勤。

def_en: microscopic organisms that can cause disease or be beneficial (harmful bacteria, gut bacteria, bacterial infection)
def_zh: 细菌（有害细菌，肠道细菌，细菌感染）
quote_en: Some bacteria help digest food, while others make you sick.
quote_zh: 有些细菌帮助消化食物，而另一些则让你生病。
ex_en: In 1928, Alexander Fleming discovered that mould could kill bacteria, leading to the first antibiotic.
ex_zh: 1928年，亚历山大·弗莱明发现霉菌可以杀死细菌，从而发明了第一种抗生素。

def_en: having the ability or skill to do something (capable of, highly capable, capable leader)
def_zh: 有能力的（能够，非常有能力，有能力的领导者）
quote_en: You are capable of achieving great things if you work hard.
quote_zh: 如果你努力，你能够取得伟大的成就。
ex_en: The ancient Roman army was capable of building a fully fortified camp in just a few hours.
ex_zh: 古罗马军队有能力在几小时内建成一个全副武装的营地。

def_en: dedicated to a cause or activity (fully committed, committed to, committed member)
def_zh: 尽心尽力的（完全投入，致力于，尽心尽力的成员）
quote_en: She is a committed volunteer who never misses a shift.
quote_zh: 她是一位尽心尽力的志愿者，从不缺席值班。
ex_en: Thomas Edison remained committed to his work, trying over 1,000 materials before finding the right one for a light bulb filament.
ex_zh: 托马斯·爱迪生始终致力于他的工作，在找到适合灯泡灯丝的材料之前尝试了1000多种材料。

def_en: having enough skill or knowledge to do something well (competent teacher, competent in, fully competent)
def_zh: 能干的（能干的老师，胜任，完全胜任）
quote_en: A competent mechanic can fix most car problems quickly.
quote_zh: 一位能干的机械师能快速解决大多数汽车问题。
ex_en: To be a competent scribe in ancient Egypt, one had to memorize over 700 hieroglyphs.
ex_zh: 要成为古埃及一位能干的抄写员，必须记住700多个象形文字。

def_en: trying to control other people's behaviour (controlling parent, controlling behaviour, overly controlling)
def_zh: 控制欲强的（控制欲强的家长，控制行为，过度控制）
quote_en: His controlling nature made it hard for his teammates to share ideas.
quote_zh: 他控制欲强的性格让队友很难分享想法。
ex_en: In George Orwell's novel *Animal Farm* (1945, public domain in some countries), Napoleon the pig becomes a controlling dictator.
ex_zh: 在乔治·奥威尔的小说《动物农场》（1945年，在一些国家属于公有领域）中，拿破仑猪成了一个控制欲强的独裁者。

def_en: costing a lot of money (expensive car, too expensive, expensive taste)
def_zh: 昂贵的（昂贵的汽车，太贵，昂贵的品味）
quote_en: Eating at that restaurant is expensive, so we only go on birthdays.
quote_zh: 在那家餐厅吃饭很贵，所以我们只在生日时才去。
ex_en: During the California Gold Rush (1848–1855), a single egg could cost the equivalent of $50 in today's money – an expensive meal.
ex_zh: 在加州淘金热期间（1848–1855），一个鸡蛋的价格相当于今天的50美元——一顿昂贵的餐食。

def_en: existing only in the mind, not real (imaginary friend, imaginary world, purely imaginary)
def_zh: 想象的（想象中的朋友，想象的世界，纯粹想象）
quote_en: The child had an imaginary dragon that lived under his bed.
quote_zh: 那个孩子有一个想象出来的龙，住在他的床底下。
ex_en: In H.G. Wells' *The War of the Worlds* (1898, public domain), Martians use imaginary technology that seemed impossible at the time.
ex_zh: 在赫伯特·乔治·威尔斯的《世界大战》（1898年，公有领域）中，火星人使用了当时看来不可能的想象出来的技术。

def_en: relating to the moon (lunar surface, lunar eclipse, lunar module)
def_zh: 月球的（月球表面，月食，登月舱）
quote_en: The astronauts brought back lunar rock samples for study.
quote_zh: 宇航员带回了月球岩石样本进行研究。
ex_en: On July 20, 1969, the lunar module Eagle landed on the Moon, and Neil Armstrong took his first steps.
ex_zh: 1969年7月20日，登月舱“鹰”号降落在月球上，尼尔·阿姆斯特朗迈出了他的第一步。

def_en: the main ways that large numbers of people receive information (social media, news media, mass media)
def_zh: 媒体（社交媒体，新闻媒体，大众媒体）
quote_en: The media reported on the election results all night.
quote_zh: 媒体整晚报道选举结果。
ex_en: In 1938, a radio drama of *The War of the Worlds* caused panic because some media listeners believed the fake news was real.
ex_zh: 1938年，广播剧《世界大战》引起了恐慌，因为一些媒体听众相信了虚假新闻。

def_en: the use of scientific knowledge for practical purposes (modern technology, digital technology, medical technology)
def_zh: 技术（现代技术，数字技术，医疗技术）
quote_en: New technology has made it possible to video call anyone in the world.
quote_zh: 新技术使得可以与世界上任何人进行视频通话。
ex_en: The invention of the printing press in 1440 was a revolutionary technology that allowed books to be mass‑produced.
ex_zh: 1440年印刷术的发明是一项革命性的技术，使书籍可以大规模生产。

def_en: the soft organ in the mouth used for tasting and speaking; a language (burn your tongue, mother tongue, tongue twister)
def_zh: 舌头（烫伤舌头，母语，绕口令）
quote_en: I bit my tongue while eating, and it hurt for days.
quote_zh: 我吃东西时咬到了舌头，疼了好几天。
ex_en: English is the official language of many countries, but it is not the native tongue of most people in the world.
ex_zh: 英语是许多国家的官方语言，但并不是世界上大多数人的母语。

def_en: an event causing great suffering or destruction (terrible tragedy, family tragedy, end in tragedy); a serious play with an unhappy ending (Greek tragedy, Shakespearean tragedy)
def_zh: 悲剧（可怕的悲剧，家庭悲剧，以悲剧告终）；悲剧作品（希腊悲剧，莎士比亚悲剧）
quote_en: The sinking of the ship was a tragedy that claimed many lives.
quote_zh: 船只沉没是一场夺走许多生命的悲剧。
ex_en: Shakespeare's *Romeo and Juliet* is a famous tragedy about two young lovers from feuding families.
ex_zh: 莎士比亚的《罗密欧与朱丽叶》是一部著名的悲剧，讲述两个来自敌对家族的年轻恋人的故事。

def_en: good enough or allowed (socially acceptable, acceptable behaviour, acceptable standard)
def_zh: 可接受的（社会上可接受的，可接受的行为，可接受的标准）
quote_en: A score of 70% is acceptable, but you can do better.
quote_zh: 70分是可以接受的，但你可以做得更好。
ex_en: In ancient Sparta, it was acceptable for girls to receive physical training – unusual for the time.
ex_zh: 在古斯巴达，女孩接受体能训练是可接受的——这在当时很不寻常。

def_en: from or in the air (aerial view, aerial photograph, aerial combat)
def_zh: 空中的（鸟瞰图，航拍照片，空战）
quote_en: The drone took an aerial shot of the entire football field.
quote_zh: 无人机拍摄了整个足球场的鸟瞰图。
ex_en: During World War I, aerial combat was new; pilots fired pistols at each other before machine guns were mounted.
ex_zh: 第一次世界大战期间，空战还是新鲜事；在安装机枪之前，飞行员互相用手枪射击。

def_en: a person who applies for something (job applicant, successful applicant, applicant for admission)
def_zh: 申请人（求职者，成功的申请人，入学申请人）
quote_en: The best applicant for the scholarship had excellent grades and community service.
quote_zh: 奖学金的最佳申请人成绩优异且做过社区服务。
ex_en: In 1963, there were over 500 applicants for just 14 astronaut positions in NASA's second group.
ex_zh: 1963年，美国宇航局第二组宇航员仅有14个职位，却有超过500名申请人。

def_en: a person who directs an orchestra (orchestra conductor, famous conductor); a person who collects fares on a bus or train (bus conductor, train conductor); a material that allows electricity or heat to pass through (electrical conductor, good conductor)
def_zh: 指挥（乐队指挥，著名指挥）；售票员（公交车售票员，火车售票员）；导体（电导体，良导体）
quote_en: The conductor raised his baton, and the orchestra began to play.
quote_zh: 指挥举起指挥棒，管弦乐队开始演奏。
ex_en: Metals like copper are excellent electrical conductors, which is why they are used in wires.
ex_zh: 铜等金属是极好的电导体，这就是为什么它们被用于电线。

def_en: advice or guidance (wise counsel, seek counsel); to give advice (counsel someone, counsel patience)
def_zh: 建议（明智的建议，寻求建议）；劝告（劝告某人，建议耐心）
quote_en: You should seek counsel from a teacher before deciding.
quote_zh: 在决定之前，你应该向老师寻求建议。
ex_en: In the Middle Ages, kings often had a trusted advisor to counsel them on matters of war and peace.
ex_zh: 中世纪时期，国王常常有一位值得信赖的顾问就战争与和平事宜向他们提出建议。

def_en: the gradual wearing away of soil or rock by wind or water (soil erosion, coastal erosion, prevent erosion)
def_zh: 侵蚀（水土流失，海岸侵蚀，防止侵蚀）
quote_en: Planting trees can help prevent soil erosion on hillsides.
quote_zh: 植树有助于防止山坡上的水土流失。
ex_en: The Grand Canyon was formed over millions of years by the erosion of the Colorado River.
ex_zh: 大峡谷是数百万年来科罗拉多河侵蚀形成的。

def_en: the state of being real or alive (human existence, come into existence, brief existence)
def_zh: 存在（人类存在，产生，短暂存在）
quote_en: Scientists have found evidence of the existence of water on Mars.
quote_zh: 科学家发现了火星上存在水的证据。
ex_en: The dinosaurs' existence ended about 65 million years ago after an asteroid struck Earth.
ex_zh: 恐龙的存在大约在6500万年前小行星撞击地球后结束。

def_en: the main office or centre of control (company headquarters, military headquarters, police headquarters)
def_zh: 总部（公司总部，军事指挥部，警察总局）
quote_en: The United Nations has its headquarters in New York City.
quote_zh: 联合国总部设在纽约市。
ex_en: During World War II, General Eisenhower's headquarters were in London before the D-Day invasion.
ex_zh: 第二次世界大战期间，艾森豪威尔将军的指挥部在诺曼底登陆前设在伦敦。

def_en: allowed by law or reasonable (legitimate reason, legitimate business, legitimate claim)
def_zh: 合法的（正当理由，合法生意，合法要求）
quote_en: He had a legitimate excuse for being late – his bus broke down.
quote_zh: 他有一个正当的迟到理由——他的公交车坏了。
ex_en: In 1688, the English Parliament decided that King James II had no legitimate right to rule, so they invited William and Mary to take the throne.
ex_zh: 1688年，英国议会判定国王詹姆斯二世没有合法的统治权，于是邀请威廉和玛丽即位。

def_en: to happen or take place (occur suddenly, occur naturally, occur to someone)
def_zh: 发生（突然发生，自然发生，某人想到）
quote_en: Earthquakes occur when tectonic plates shift underground.
quote_zh: 当地下板块移动时就会发生地震。
ex_en: The idea of the light bulb occurred to Thomas Edison while he was experimenting with electrical currents.
ex_zh: 电灯泡的想法是托马斯·爱迪生在实验电流时想到的。

def_en: a substance found in foods like meat, eggs, and beans that is essential for growth (high protein, protein source, protein shake)
def_zh: 蛋白质（高蛋白，蛋白质来源，蛋白质奶昔）
quote_en: Chicken and fish are excellent sources of protein.
quote_zh: 鸡肉和鱼是极好的蛋白质来源。
ex_en: In 1838, Dutch chemist Gerardus Mulder first described protein, calling it the "primary substance" of life.
ex_zh: 1838年，荷兰化学家杰拉德斯·穆尔德首次描述了蛋白质，称其为生命的“主要物质”。

def_en: the importance or meaning of something (great significance, historical significance, attach significance)
def_zh: 重要性（重大意义，历史意义，重视）
quote_en: The discovery of penicillin was of great significance to medicine.
quote_zh: 青霉素的发现对医学具有重大意义。
ex_en: The signing of the Magna Carta in 1215 had huge historical significance because it limited the king's power.
ex_zh: 1215年《大宪章》的签署具有巨大的历史意义，因为它限制了国王的权力。

def_en: alone or done alone (solitary life, solitary confinement, solitary walk)
def_zh: 独自的（独居生活，单独监禁，独自散步）
quote_en: The old man led a solitary life in a cabin in the woods.
quote_zh: 那位老人住在林中小屋里过着独居生活。
ex_en: Henry David Thoreau lived a solitary life for two years at Walden Pond, which he wrote about in his famous book.
ex_zh: 亨利·戴维·梭罗在瓦尔登湖过了两年独居生活，并将其写进了他的名著。

def_en: a large ship or boat (sailing vessel, cargo vessel, naval vessel); a container for liquids (clay vessel, drinking vessel)
def_zh: 船只（帆船，货船，军舰）；容器（陶罐，饮水器皿）
quote_en: The ancient vessel carried spices from India to Rome.
quote_zh: 那艘古老的船只从印度向罗马运送香料。
ex_en: In 1492, Christopher Columbus's three vessels – the Niña, the Pinta, and the Santa María – crossed the Atlantic.
ex_zh: 1492年，克里斯托弗·哥伦布的三艘船——尼尼亚号、平塔号和圣玛丽亚号——横渡了大西洋。

def_en: the act of breaking a law or rule (traffic violation, human rights violation, violation of agreement)
def_zh: 违反（交通违规，侵犯人权，违反协议）
quote_en: Running a red light is a serious traffic violation.
quote_zh: 闯红灯是严重的交通违规。
ex_en: In 1773, the colonists saw the Tea Act as a violation of their rights because they had no representation in Parliament.
ex_zh: 1773年，殖民者将《茶叶法案》视为对其权利的侵犯，因为他们在议会中没有代表。

def_en: something successfully achieved (great accomplishment, sense of accomplishment, remarkable accomplishment)
def_zh: 成就（伟大成就，成就感，非凡成就）
quote_en: Graduating from primary school was a big accomplishment for him.
quote_zh: 小学毕业对他来说是一个巨大的成就。
ex_en: The building of the transcontinental railroad in 1869 was an incredible accomplishment, linking the east and west coasts of America.
ex_zh: 1869年横贯大陆铁路的建成是一项令人难以置信的成就，连接了美国东西海岸。

def_en: a knitted blanket or shawl (crocheted afghan, wool afghan)
def_zh: 针织毯（钩针编织的毯子，羊毛毯）
quote_en: Grandma knitted a warm afghan for the living room sofa.
quote_zh: 奶奶为客厅沙发织了一条温暖的针织毯。
ex_en: Afghan blankets became popular in the Victorian era when knitting became a fashionable hobby for women.
ex_zh: 针织毯在维多利亚时代流行起来，当时编织成为女性时尚的爱好。

def_en: a black, sticky substance used for making roads (asphalt pavement, asphalt road, asphalt plant)
def_zh: 沥青（沥青路面，沥青路，沥青厂）
quote_en: The workers laid hot asphalt to repair the pothole.
quote_zh: 工人们铺设热沥青来修补坑洞。
ex_en: The first asphalt road in the United States was laid in Newark, New Jersey, in 1870.
ex_zh: 美国第一条沥青路于1870年铺设在新泽西州的纽瓦克。

def_en: a vegetable with long crisp green stalks (celery stick, celery stalk, celery salt)
def_zh: 芹菜（芹菜条，芹菜茎，芹菜盐）
quote_en: Peanut butter on celery makes a healthy snack.
quote_zh: 芹菜上抹花生酱是一种健康的零食。
ex_en: Ancient Romans used celery leaves to make crowns for winners of athletic contests, similar to laurel wreaths.
ex_zh: 古罗马人用芹菜叶为体育比赛的获胜者制作冠冕，类似于月桂花环。

def_en: to complete or add to something in a way that improves it (complement each other, perfect complement, wine complement)
def_zh: 补充；衬托（互相补充，完美补充，搭配）
quote_en: The spicy sauce complements the grilled chicken perfectly.
quote_zh: 辣酱完美地衬托了烤鸡。
ex_en: In colour theory, red and green complement each other, which is why they are used so often in art and design.
ex_zh: 在色彩理论中，红色和绿色互为补色，这就是它们经常在艺术和设计中使用的缘故。

def_en: words expressing praise for an achievement (heartfelt congratulations, offer congratulations, congratulations on)
def_zh: 祝贺（衷心的祝贺，致以祝贺，祝贺……）
quote_en: Congratulations on winning the spelling bee! You earned it.
quote_zh: 祝贺你在拼写比赛中获胜！这是你应得的。
ex_en: When Thomas Edison invented the phonograph in 1877, he received congratulations from people all over the world, including President Hayes.
ex_zh: 当托马斯·爱迪生于1877年发明留声机时，他收到了来自世界各地（包括海斯总统）的祝贺。

def_en: the act of sending someone away or firing them from a job (unfair dismissal, dismissal from school, dismissal of a case)
def_zh: 解雇；驳回（不公正解雇，开除学籍，案件驳回）
quote_en: The teacher's dismissal of the class was delayed by ten minutes.
quote_zh: 老师推迟了十分钟才下课。
ex_en: In 1962, the dismissal of a Soviet officer named Stanislav Petrov from his job (for other reasons) might have saved the world – he once ignored a false alarm that could have started a nuclear war.
ex_zh: 1962年，一位名叫斯坦尼斯拉夫·彼得罗夫的苏联军官被解职（因其他原因）可能拯救了世界——他曾忽略了一次可能引发核战争的假警报。

def_en: to a very high degree (extremely hot, extremely difficult, extremely important)
def_zh: 极其（极其炎热，极其困难，极其重要）
quote_en: The test was extremely hard, but I studied enough to pass.
quote_zh: 考试极其困难，但我学得足够好通过了。
ex_en: The Wright brothers' first flight in 1903 was extremely short – only 12 seconds – but it changed human history.
ex_zh: 莱特兄弟1903年的第一次飞行极其短暂——仅12秒——却改变了人类历史。

def_en: very brave or admired (heroic effort, heroic act, heroic figure)
def_zh: 英勇的（英勇的努力，英勇的行为，英雄人物）
quote_en: The firefighter made a heroic rescue, saving a family from the burning house.
quote_zh: 消防员进行了英勇的救援，从燃烧的房子里救出了一家人。
ex_en: During World War II, the heroic actions of ordinary citizens in the French Resistance helped save many lives.
ex_zh: 第二次世界大战期间，法国抵抗运动中普通公民的英勇行动帮助挽救了许多生命。

def_en: to reduce something to the smallest possible amount (minimize risk, minimize damage, minimize waste)
def_zh: 最小化（最小化风险，最小化损害，减少浪费）
quote_en: You can minimize your mistakes by checking your work twice.
quote_zh: 通过检查两遍作业，你可以最大限度地减少错误。
ex_en: To minimize the spread of disease during the 1854 cholera outbreak, Dr. John Snow removed a contaminated pump handle.
ex_zh: 为了最大限度地减少1854年霍乱疫情期间疾病的传播，约翰·斯诺医生移除了一个被污染的水泵手柄。

def_en: the set of characteristics that make a person unique (strong personality, cheerful personality, personality disorder)
def_zh: 个性（坚强的个性，开朗的性格，人格障碍）
quote_en: Her bubbly personality makes her popular with everyone.
quote_zh: 她活泼的个性使她深受大家欢迎。
ex_en: Psychologist Sigmund Freud proposed that personality is made up of three parts: the id, ego, and superego.
ex_zh: 心理学家西格蒙德·弗洛伊德提出，人格由三部分组成：本我、自我和超我。

def_en: having the necessary qualities or meeting the standards (qualifying exam, qualifying round, qualifying score)
def_zh: 预选的；使具有资格的（资格考试，预选赛，及格分数）
quote_en: She ran the qualifying race in record time and advanced to the finals.
quote_zh: 她以创纪录的时间跑完了预选赛，进入了决赛。
ex_en: In ancient Greece, athletes had to swear a qualifying oath before competing in the Olympic Games.
ex_zh: 在古希腊，运动员在参加奥运会之前必须宣誓通过资格审核。

def_en: a person who handles correspondence and records for an organization (executive secretary, company secretary, secretary of state)
def_zh: 秘书（行政秘书，公司秘书，国务卿）
quote_en: The secretary typed the meeting minutes and sent them to all members.
quote_zh: 秘书打出了会议记录并发送给所有成员。
ex_en: The first woman to serve as U.S. Secretary of State was Madeleine Albright, appointed in 1997.
ex_zh: 第一位担任美国国务卿的女性是马德琳·奥尔布赖特，于1997年被任命。

def_en: in a genuine and honest way (sincerely hope, sincerely believe, yours sincerely)
def_zh: 真诚地（真诚希望，真诚相信，此致敬礼）
quote_en: I sincerely apologize for breaking your favourite mug.
quote_zh: 我真诚地为打碎你最喜欢的杯子道歉。
ex_en: Abraham Lincoln was known for speaking sincerely; his Gettysburg Address is only 272 words but deeply moving.
ex_zh: 亚伯拉罕·林肯以真诚的讲话而闻名；他的葛底斯堡演说只有272个单词，却感人至深。

def_en: cruel and oppressive rule by a single leader (political tyranny, escape tyranny, tyranny of the majority)
def_zh: 暴政（政治暴政，逃离暴政，多数人的暴政）
quote_en: The citizens rose up against the king's tyranny and demanded freedom.
quote_zh: 公民们奋起反抗国王的暴政，要求自由。
ex_en: The American colonists believed that King George III's rule was a tyranny, which motivated them to declare independence in 1776.
ex_zh: 美国殖民者认为乔治三世国王的统治是暴政，这促使他们于1776年宣布独立。

def_en: to gather or build up over time (accumulate wealth, accumulate dust, accumulate evidence)
def_zh: 积累（积累财富，积满灰尘，积累证据）
quote_en: If you don't clean your room, clutter will accumulate quickly.
quote_zh: 如果你不打扫房间，杂物很快就会堆积起来。
ex_en: Over millions of years, layers of dead plants accumulated and turned into coal, a fossil fuel.
ex_zh: 数百万年来，一层层死去的植物积累起来变成了煤炭——一种化石燃料。

def_en: a person who designs buildings (famous architect, chief architect, landscape architect)
def_zh: 建筑师（著名建筑师，首席建筑师，景观建筑师）
quote_en: The architect drew up plans for a new school library.
quote_zh: 建筑师为一所新的学校图书馆绘制了计划。
ex_en: Frank Lloyd Wright, one of America's most famous architects, designed the Guggenheim Museum in New York.
ex_zh: 弗兰克·劳埃德·赖特是美国最著名的建筑师之一，设计了纽约的古根海姆博物馆。

def_en: happening every two months or twice a month (bimonthly meeting, bimonthly magazine, paid bimonthly)
def_zh: 每两月一次的；一月两次的（双月刊，每两月一次的会议，每半月发薪）
quote_en: Our club holds bimonthly meetings – one in January, one in March, and so on.
quote_zh: 我们俱乐部每两月开一次会——一月一次，三月一次，依此类推。
ex_en: Some employees are paid bimonthly (twice a month), while others are paid every two weeks.
ex_zh: 有些员工每月领两次工资，而另一些则每两周领一次。

def_en: public disagreement or debate (heated controversy, cause controversy, avoid controversy)
def_zh: 争议（激烈的争论，引起争议，避免争议）
quote_en: The new law caused great controversy among citizens.
quote_zh: 新法律在公民中引起了巨大争议。
ex_en: The discovery of the Dead Sea Scrolls in 1947 led to decades of controversy among scholars about their origin.
ex_zh: 1947年死海古卷的发现导致了学者们对其起源长达数十年的争议。

def_en: wanted or worth having (highly desirable, desirable location, desirable qualities)
def_zh: 值得拥有的（非常值得拥有，理想的位置，可取的品质）
quote_en: A quiet neighbourhood near a park is very desirable for families.
quote_zh: 公园附近安静的社区对家庭来说非常理想。
ex_en: In the California Gold Rush, owning a pickaxe and a pan was more desirable than owning a fancy suit.
ex_zh: 在加州淘金热期间，拥有镐和淘金盘比拥有花哨的西装更值得。

def_en: the identification of an illness or problem (medical diagnosis, early diagnosis, accurate diagnosis)
def_zh: 诊断（医学诊断，早期诊断，准确诊断）
quote_en: The doctor's diagnosis was a broken wrist, so they put a cast on it.
quote_zh: 医生的诊断是手腕骨折，所以他们打上了石膏。
ex_en: In 1854, Dr. John Snow's diagnosis of cholera as a waterborne disease changed public health forever.
ex_zh: 1854年，约翰·斯诺医生将霍乱诊断为水源性疾病，永远改变了公共卫生。

def_en: to make someone less confident or willing to do something (discourage from trying, easily discouraged, discourage competition)
def_zh: 使气馁（使不敢尝试，容易气馁，阻止竞争）
quote_en: Don't let one bad grade discourage you from studying harder.
quote_zh: 不要因为一次糟糕的成绩就气馁而不愿更加努力。
ex_en: The harsh winter discouraged many early Jamestown settlers, but some persevered and survived.
ex_zh: 严冬使许多早期的詹姆斯敦定居者气馁，但有些人坚持了下来。

def_en: to put out a fire or light (extinguish flames, extinguish a candle, extinguish hope)
def_zh: 熄灭（扑灭火焰，熄灭蜡烛，破灭希望）
quote_en: Firefighters worked for hours to extinguish the forest fire.
quote_zh: 消防员花了数小时才扑灭森林大火。
ex_en: In 1666, the Great Fire of London was finally extinguished after burning for four days and destroying over 13,000 houses.
ex_zh: 1666年，伦敦大火在燃烧了四天、摧毁了13000多座房屋后才最终被扑灭。

def_en: to prevent something from moving (immobilize a joint, immobilize a vehicle, immobilize a criminal)
def_zh: 使不动（固定关节，使车辆无法移动，使罪犯无法动弹）
quote_en: The paramedic used a splint to immobilize the broken leg.
quote_zh: 医护人员用夹板固定了骨折的腿。
ex_en: During World War I, tanks were used to break through enemy lines, but mud often immobilized them.
ex_zh: 第一次世界大战期间，坦克被用来突破敌方防线，但泥浆常常使它们动弹不得。

def_en: hopeful and confident about the future (optimistic outlook, remain optimistic, overly optimistic)
def_zh: 乐观的（乐观前景，保持乐观，过于乐观）
quote_en: Even when losing, the coach remained optimistic about the next game.
quote_zh: 即使输球，教练对下一场比赛仍然保持乐观。
ex_en: Despite the Great Depression, many Americans remained optimistic, believing that hard work would lead to recovery.
ex_zh: 尽管大萧条时期，许多美国人仍然保持乐观，相信努力工作会带来复苏。

def_en: a device with blades that spins to push a vehicle forward (airplane propeller, ship propeller, propeller blade)
def_zh: 螺旋桨（飞机螺旋桨，船用螺旋桨，螺旋桨叶片）
quote_en: The boat's propeller got tangled in seaweed and stopped working.
quote_zh: 船的螺旋桨被海藻缠住，停止了工作。
ex_en: The Wright brothers' 1903 Flyer used two wooden propellers connected by a chain – a design inspired by bicycles.
ex_zh: 莱特兄弟1903年的飞行者号使用了两根链条连接的木制螺旋桨——这一设计灵感来自自行车。

def_en: small and weak (puny arms, puny effort, puny child)
def_zh: 弱小的（细弱的胳膊，微不足道的努力，瘦弱的孩子）
quote_en: The puny kitten was much smaller than its siblings.
quote_zh: 那只瘦弱的小猫比它的兄弟姐妹小得多。
ex_en: When the first Model T Ford was introduced, it looked puny compared to horse‑drawn carriages, but it changed transportation forever.
ex_zh: 第一辆福特T型车推出时，与马车相比显得很小，但它永远改变了交通。

def_en: a person who studies a subject in depth (history scholar, research scholar, distinguished scholar)
def_zh: 学者（历史学者，研究学者，杰出学者）
quote_en: The young scholar won an award for her essay on ancient Rome.
quote_zh: 那位年轻学者以关于古罗马的文章获奖。
ex_en: Ibn al-Haytham, a 10th-century Islamic scholar, is considered the father of the scientific method.
ex_zh: 10世纪的伊斯兰学者伊本·海赛姆被认为是科学方法之父。

def_en: having a lot of space (spacious room, spacious kitchen, spacious garden)
def_zh: 宽敞的（宽敞的房间，宽敞的厨房，宽敞的花园）
quote_en: The new classroom is much more spacious than the old one.
quote_zh: 新教室比旧教室宽敞得多。
ex_en: The ancient Roman baths were incredibly spacious, with some able to hold over 1,500 people at once.
ex_zh: 古罗马浴场非常宽敞，有些能同时容纳1500多人。

def_en: feeling that something is wrong or that someone is guilty (suspicious behaviour, suspicious of, look suspicious)
def_zh: 怀疑的（可疑行为，怀疑，看起来可疑）
quote_en: The police officer became suspicious when the man gave a false name.
quote_zh: 当那个男人报出假名字时，警察变得怀疑起来。
ex_en: In 1692, suspicious behaviour in Salem, Massachusetts, led to accusations of witchcraft and the infamous witch trials.
ex_zh: 1692年，马萨诸塞州塞勒姆的可疑行为导致了巫术指控和臭名昭著的女巫审判。

def_en: enough or sufficient for a purpose (adequate supply, adequate preparation, barely adequate)
def_zh: 足够的（充足的供应，充分的准备，勉强够）
quote_en: A glass of water is adequate for taking a pill, but you need more for exercise.
quote_zh: 一杯水足以服药，但运动需要更多。
ex_en: In 1804, Meriwether Lewis considered the supplies for the expedition barely adequate, but they managed to survive.
ex_zh: 1804年，梅里韦瑟·刘易斯认为远征的物资勉强够用，但他们还是设法生存了下来。

def_en: a small tube at the end of the large intestine (ruptured appendix, appendix removal); a section of extra information at the end of a book (see appendix, appendix includes)
def_zh: 阑尾（阑尾破裂，阑尾切除）；附录（见附录，附录包括）
quote_en: The doctor said his appendix was inflamed and needed surgery.
quote_zh: 医生说他的阑尾发炎了，需要手术。
ex_en: The first edition of *The Hobbit* (1937) had an appendix that explained the runes used by the dwarves.
ex_zh: 1937年第一版《霍比特人》有一个附录，解释了矮人使用的如尼文字。

def_en: happening twice a year (biannual meeting, biannual check-up, biannual report)
def_zh: 一年两次的（一年两次的会议，一年两次的体检，一年两次的报告）
quote_en: The team holds a biannual planning session in January and July.
quote_zh: 团队在一月和七月举行一年两次的规划会议。
ex_en: Many companies publish biannual financial reports, one in spring and one in autumn.
ex_zh: 许多公司发布一年两次的财务报告，春季一次，秋季一次。

def_en: spreading easily from person to person (contagious disease, highly contagious, contagious laugh)
def_zh: 传染性的（传染病，高度传染性，有感染力的笑声）
quote_en: The flu is contagious, so stay home if you are sick.
quote_zh: 流感具有传染性，所以如果你生病了就待在家里。
ex_en: The 1918 Spanish flu was extremely contagious, infecting about one‑third of the world's population.
ex_zh: 1918年的西班牙流感极具传染性，感染了世界约三分之一的人口。

def_en: something that discourages or prevents an action (crime deterrent, nuclear deterrent, effective deterrent)
def_zh: 威慑（犯罪威慑，核威慑，有效的威慑）
quote_en: A visible security camera can be a strong deterrent to shoplifters.
quote_zh: 可见的监控摄像头对商店扒手是一个强大的威慑。
ex_en: The Great Wall of China was built as a deterrent against invaders, but it was not always successful.
ex_zh: 中国的长城是作为抵御入侵者的威慑而建造的，但并不总是成功。

def_en: a difficult choice between two options (ethical dilemma, face a dilemma, resolve a dilemma)
def_zh: 困境（道德困境，面临困境，解决困境）
quote_en: She faced a dilemma: tell the truth and get in trouble, or lie and feel guilty.
quote_zh: 她面临一个困境：说实话会惹麻烦，或者说谎会感到内疚。
ex_en: During the Cold War, President Kennedy faced a dilemma: attack Cuba and risk nuclear war, or negotiate and appear weak.
ex_zh: 冷战期间，肯尼迪总统面临一个困境：进攻古巴并冒核战争的风险，或者谈判并显得软弱。

def_en: the surroundings in which a person, animal, or plant lives (natural environment, learning environment, protect the environment)
def_zh: 环境（自然环境，学习环境，保护环境）
quote_en: A clean and quiet environment helps students focus on their work.
quote_zh: 干净、安静的环境有助于学生专注于功课。
ex_en: In 1970, the first Earth Day was celebrated to raise awareness about protecting the environment.
ex_zh: 1970年，第一个地球日是为了提高保护环境的意识而庆祝的。

def_en: often or many times (frequently asked, occur frequently, frequently change)
def_zh: 频繁地（常见问题，频繁发生，频繁变化）
quote_en: He frequently visits his grandmother after school.
quote_zh: 他放学后经常去看望奶奶。
ex_en: Earthquakes occur frequently along the Pacific Ring of Fire, which surrounds the Pacific Ocean.
ex_zh: 沿着环太平洋火山带，地震频繁发生，该火山带环绕太平洋。

def_en: demanding something firmly and repeatedly (insistent voice, insistent on, be insistent)
def_zh: 坚持的（坚持的声音，坚持要，执意）
quote_en: The child was insistent that he had seen a ghost in the attic.
quote_zh: 那个孩子坚持说他在阁楼里看到了鬼。
ex_en: In 1848, James Marshall was insistent that he had found gold at Sutter's Mill, but no one believed him until later.
ex_zh: 1848年，詹姆斯·马歇尔坚持说他在萨特的磨坊发现了黄金，但直到后来才有人相信他。

def_en: to question someone closely and formally (interrogate a suspect, interrogate prisoners, harshly interrogate)
def_zh: 审问（审问嫌疑人，审问囚犯，严厉审问）
quote_en: The detective had to interrogate the witness to get the full story.
quote_zh: 侦探不得不审问目击者以获取全部真相。
ex_en: During World War II, Allied soldiers were trained to resist being interrogated by the enemy if captured.
ex_zh: 第二次世界大战期间，盟军士兵接受了在被俘时抵制敌人审问的训练。

def_en: the head of a school (school principal, assistant principal, principal's office)
def_zh: 校长（学校校长，副校长，校长办公室）
quote_en: The principal announced a new dress code at morning assembly.
quote_zh: 校长在晨会上宣布了新的着装要求。
ex_en: The first public school principal in the United States was appointed in Boston in 1789.
ex_zh: 美国第一位公立学校校长于1789年在波士顿任命。

def_en: clean and healthy (sanitary conditions, sanitary facilities, sanitary pad)
def_zh: 卫生的（卫生条件，卫生设施，卫生巾）
quote_en: The restaurant was closed because the kitchen was not sanitary.
quote_zh: 那家餐厅因厨房不卫生而被关闭。
ex_en: Florence Nightingale improved sanitary conditions in military hospitals, reducing the death rate from 42% to 2%.
ex_zh: 弗洛伦斯·南丁格尔改善了军队医院的卫生条件，将死亡率从42%降至2%。

def_en: a second-year student in high school or college (high school sophomore, sophomore year, sophomore class)
def_zh: 二年级学生（高中二年级学生，二年级，二年级班级）
quote_en: As a sophomore, he finally felt comfortable navigating the school.
quote_zh: 作为二年级学生，他终于对在校园里走动感到自如了。
ex_en: The word "sophomore" comes from Greek words meaning "wise fool" – because second-year students think they know everything but still have much to learn.
ex_zh: “sophomore”一词来自希腊语，意为“聪明的傻瓜”——因为二年级学生认为自己什么都知道，但仍有很多东西要学。

def_en: relating to a residential area outside a city (suburban house, suburban life, suburban neighbourhood)
def_zh: 郊区的（郊区房屋，郊区生活，郊区社区）
quote_en: They moved from a crowded city apartment to a quiet suburban home.
quote_zh: 他们从拥挤的城市公寓搬到了安静的郊区住宅。
ex_en: After World War II, many American families moved to suburban communities like Levittown, which were built quickly for returning soldiers.
ex_zh: 第二次世界大战后，许多美国家庭搬到了像莱维敦这样的郊区社区，这些社区是为回国的士兵快速建造的。

def_en: a social or religious custom forbidding something (cultural taboo, break a taboo, taboo subject)
def_zh: 禁忌（文化禁忌，打破禁忌，禁忌话题）
quote_en: In some cultures, it is taboo to point your feet at another person.
quote_zh: 在某些文化中，把脚指向别人是禁忌。
ex_en: In ancient Hawaii, it was taboo for women to eat certain foods like bananas and coconuts – a rule broken by King Kamehameha II in 1819.
ex_zh: 在古代夏威夷，妇女吃香蕉和椰子等某些食物是禁忌——1819年卡美哈梅哈二世国王打破了这一规则。

def_en: a person from whom one is descended (direct ancestor, common ancestor, ancestor worship)
def_zh: 祖先（直系祖先，共同祖先，祖先崇拜）
quote_en: My ancestors came to America from Ireland in the 1800s.
quote_zh: 我的祖先在十九世纪从爱尔兰来到美国。
ex_en: Scientists believe that humans and chimpanzees share a common ancestor that lived about 6 million years ago.
ex_zh: 科学家认为人类和黑猩猩有一个生活在大约600万年前的共同祖先。

def_en: feeling worried or nervous (anxious about, anxious wait, anxious face)
def_zh: 焦虑的（为……焦虑，焦急等待，焦虑的表情）
quote_en: I felt anxious before the big test, but I passed.
quote_zh: 大考前我感到焦虑，但我通过了。
ex_en: During the Cuban Missile Crisis in 1962, millions of Americans were anxious about the possibility of nuclear war.
ex_zh: 1962年古巴导弹危机期间，数百万美国人对核战争的可能性感到焦虑。

def_en: relating to the story of a person's life (biographical information, biographical details, biographical film)
def_zh: 传记的（传记信息，生平细节，传记电影）
quote_en: The library has a section for biographical books about famous people.
quote_zh: 图书馆有一个关于名人的传记书籍专区。
ex_en: James Boswell wrote a famous biographical work about Samuel Johnson, considered one of the best biographies in English.
ex_zh: 詹姆斯·博斯韦尔写了一部关于塞缪尔·约翰逊的著名传记作品，被认为是最好的英文传记之一。

def_en: without stopping or interruption (continuous line, continuous improvement, continuous rain)
def_zh: 连续的（连续线，持续改进，连绵的雨）
quote_en: The rain was continuous for three days, flooding the streets.
quote_zh: 雨连续下了三天，街道被淹。
ex_en: The Great Wall of China is not actually a continuous wall but a series of sections built over many dynasties.
ex_zh: 中国的长城实际上并不是一堵连续的墙，而是许多朝代建造的一系列段落。

def_en: a detailed evaluation or criticism (book critique, harsh critique, constructive critique)
def_zh: 评论（书评，严厉批评，建设性批评）
quote_en: The teacher wrote a helpful critique of my essay.
quote_zh: 老师对我的文章写了有帮助的评语。
ex_en: In ancient Greece, Aristotle wrote a famous critique of Plato's theory of forms, arguing that the physical world is real.
ex_zh: 在古希腊，亚里士多德写了一篇著名的对柏拉图形式论的批判，认为物质世界是真实的。

def_en: the farthest point or limit (northern extremity, extreme extremity, extremities of the body)
def_zh: 末端（最北端，极限，四肢）
quote_en: The ship reached the western extremity of the continent after weeks at sea.
quote_zh: 经过数周航行，船只到达了大陆的最西端。
ex_en: In 1911, Roald Amundsen's expedition reached the southern extremity of the Earth – the South Pole – before Robert Scott.
ex_zh: 1911年，罗尔德·阿蒙森的探险队比罗伯特·斯科特先到达了地球的最南端——南极。

def_en: lack of success (complete failure, fear of failure, crop failure)
def_zh: 失败（彻底失败，害怕失败，庄稼歉收）
quote_en: The rocket launch was a failure, but the team learned from it.
quote_zh: 火箭发射失败了，但团队从中吸取了教训。
ex_en: Thomas Edison considered each unsuccessful attempt a success because it ruled out one way that wouldn't work.
ex_zh: 托马斯·爱迪生认为每一次失败的尝试都是成功，因为它排除了一种行不通的方法。

def_en: causing trouble or difficulty (inconvenient time, inconvenient location, highly inconvenient)
def_zh: 不方便的（不方便的时间，不便的位置，极其不便）
quote_en: It's inconvenient that the shop closes at 5 PM, right when school ends.
quote_zh: 商店下午5点关门，正好学校放学，这很不方便。
ex_en: In medieval times, carrying heavy coins was inconvenient, which led to the invention of paper money in China.
ex_zh: 在中世纪，携带沉重的硬币很不方便，这导致了中国纸币的发明。

def_en: lacking skill or ability (socially inept, politically inept, inept attempt)
def_zh: 无能的（社交无能，政治上无能，无能的尝试）
quote_en: His inept attempt at fixing the sink made the leak worse.
quote_zh: 他修理水槽的无能尝试使漏水更严重了。
ex_en: The 1915 Gallipoli campaign in World War I was considered ineptly planned, with many soldiers losing their lives on the wrong beaches.
ex_zh: 第一次世界大战中1915年的加里波利战役被认为计划不周，许多士兵在错误的海滩上丧生。

def_en: to capture someone's complete attention (mesmerize the audience, completely mesmerized, mesmerize by magic)
def_zh: 迷住（迷住观众，完全着迷，被魔法迷住）
quote_en: The magician's tricks mesmerized the young children.
quote_zh: 魔术师的戏法迷住了小孩子们。
ex_en: The name "mesmerize" comes from Franz Mesmer, an 18th-century doctor who believed he could cure people with animal magnetism.
ex_zh: “mesmerize”一词来自弗朗茨·梅斯梅尔，一位18世纪的医生，他认为可以用动物磁力治愈人们。

def_en: a special right or advantage (great privilege, driving privilege, exclusive privilege)
def_zh: 特权（极大的特权，驾驶特权，专属特权）
quote_en: It was a privilege to meet the famous author and ask her questions.
quote_zh: 见到那位著名作家并向她提问是一种荣幸。
ex_en: In 1920, the 19th Amendment gave American women the privilege to vote – a right they had fought for decades.
ex_zh: 1920年，第十九条修正案赋予了美国女性投票的特权——这是她们奋斗了几十年的权利。

def_en: a safe place (wildlife sanctuary, bird sanctuary, seek sanctuary)
def_zh: 庇护所（野生动物保护区，鸟类保护区，寻求庇护）
quote_en: The old library was a quiet sanctuary for students who needed to study.
quote_zh: 旧图书馆是需要学习的学生的安静庇护所。
ex_en: In medieval churches, criminals could seek sanctuary and not be arrested for up to 40 days.
ex_zh: 在中世纪的教堂里，罪犯可以寻求庇护，最多40天不被逮捕。

def_en: a plan or system (colour scheme, payment scheme, get-rich-quick scheme)
def_zh: 计划（配色方案，支付计划，快速致富计划）
quote_en: The team came up with a clever scheme to raise money for charity.
quote_zh: 团队想出了一个聪明的计划为慈善筹款。
ex_en: The Marshall Plan was a US scheme to rebuild European economies after World War II, spending over $13 billion.
ex_zh: 马歇尔计划是美国在二战后重建欧洲经济的计划，花费了超过130亿美元。

def_en: a tool with mesh for separating solids from liquids or fine particles from coarse (kitchen sieve, flour sieve, sieve mesh)
def_zh: 筛子（厨房筛子，面粉筛，筛网）
quote_en: Use a sieve to remove lumps from the flour before baking.
quote_zh: 烘焙前用筛子去除面粉中的结块。
ex_en: The ancient Egyptians used sieves to separate gold dust from sand during their gold mining operations.
ex_zh: 古埃及人在采金作业中使用筛子将金粉从沙子中分离出来。

def_en: not able to be predicted (unpredictable weather, unpredictable behaviour, unpredictable outcome)
def_zh: 不可预测的（不可预测的天气，不可预测的行为，不可预测的结果）
quote_en: The weather in spring is unpredictable – it could snow or be sunny.
quote_zh: 春天的天气难以预测——可能下雪也可能晴朗。
ex_en: The stock market crash of 1929 showed that the economy was unpredictable, leading to the Great Depression.
ex_zh: 1929年的股市崩盘表明经济是不可预测的，导致了大萧条。

def_en: involving a benefit or advantage (advantageous position, advantageous deal, mutually advantageous)
def_zh: 有利的（有利位置，有利交易，互利共赢）
quote_en: It is advantageous to learn a second language when you are young.
quote_zh: 从小学习第二语言是有利的。
ex_en: In 1803, the Louisiana Purchase was highly advantageous for the United States, doubling its size for only $15 million.
ex_zh: 1803年，路易斯安那购地对美国非常有利，仅以1500万美元就将国土面积扩大了一倍。

def_en: not professional; a person who does something as a hobby rather than a job (amateur photographer, amateur sports, amateur musician)
def_zh: 业余的；业余爱好者（业余摄影师，业余运动，业余音乐家）
quote_en: He is an amateur painter, but his work looks professional.
quote_zh: 他是一名业余画家，但他的作品看起来很专业。
ex_en: The modern Olympic Games originally allowed only amateur athletes, but that rule changed in 1986.
ex_zh: 现代奥运会最初只允许业余运动员参加，但这一规则在1986年改变了。

def_en: an instrument measuring air pressure, used to predict weather (mercury barometer, aneroid barometer, rising barometer)
def_zh: 气压计（水银气压计，无液气压计，气压上升）
quote_en: The barometer showed falling pressure, meaning a storm was coming.
quote_zh: 气压计显示气压下降，意味着暴风雨即将来临。
ex_en: Evangelista Torricelli invented the first barometer in 1643, calling it a "barometer" from Greek words for "weight measure".
ex_zh: 埃万杰利斯塔·托里拆利于1643年发明了第一个气压计，用希腊语“重量测量”一词命名为“barometer”。

def_en: a small role in a film or play (cameo appearance, brief cameo, famous cameo)
def_zh: 客串（客串出演，短暂客串，著名客串）
quote_en: The director made a cameo in the movie as a bus driver.
quote_zh: 导演在电影中客串了一名公交车司机。
ex_en: Alfred Hitchcock was famous for making a brief cameo in almost every one of his films.
ex_zh: 阿尔弗雷德·希区柯克以几乎在他每一部电影中短暂客串而闻名。

def_en: extreme tiredness (mental fatigue, muscle fatigue, combat fatigue)
def_zh: 疲劳（精神疲劳，肌肉疲劳，战斗疲劳）
quote_en: After the long hike, fatigue set in and we had to rest.
quote_zh: 长途徒步后，疲劳袭来，我们不得不休息。
ex_en: During World War I, soldiers suffered from "shell shock" – a form of extreme fatigue caused by constant stress.
ex_zh: 第一次世界大战期间，士兵们患有“炮弹休克”——一种由持续压力引起的极度疲劳。

def_en: a complete failure (total fiasco, embarrassing fiasco, military fiasco)
def_zh: 惨败（彻底失败，令人尴尬的惨败，军事惨败）
quote_en: The school play was a fiasco when the lead actor forgot all his lines.
quote_zh: 当主角忘记所有台词时，学校戏剧成了一场惨败。
ex_en: The Bay of Pigs invasion in 1961 was a major fiasco for the US government, with the invading force captured within days.
ex_zh: 1961年的猪湾入侵是美国政府的一场重大惨败，入侵部队在几天内被俘。

def_en: damaged by extreme cold (frostbitten fingers, frostbitten toes, frostbitten skin)
def_zh: 冻伤的（冻伤的手指，冻伤的脚趾，冻伤的皮肤）
quote_en: The climber's nose was frostbitten after hours in the freezing wind.
quote_zh: 登山者的鼻子在寒风中数小时后冻伤了。
ex_en: In 1912, Captain Scott's Antarctic expedition suffered from frostbitten limbs, and all members perished on the return journey.
ex_zh: 1912年，斯科特船长的南极探险队遭受了四肢冻伤，所有成员在返程中丧生。

def_en: real and authentic (genuine leather, genuine interest, genuine apology)
def_zh: 真正的（真皮，真正的兴趣，真诚的道歉）
quote_en: That diamond ring is not genuine – it's made of glass.
quote_zh: 那枚钻石戒指不是真的——它是玻璃做的。
ex_en: The Shroud of Turin is claimed to be the genuine burial cloth of Jesus, but carbon dating suggests it is from the Middle Ages.
ex_zh: 都灵裹尸布据称是耶稣真正的裹尸布，但碳定年法表明它来自中世纪。

def_en: writing or drawings on public surfaces (illegal graffiti, graffiti artist, graffiti tag)
def_zh: 涂鸦（非法涂鸦，涂鸦艺术家，涂鸦签名）
quote_en: The train was covered in colourful graffiti.
quote_zh: 火车上布满了色彩斑斓的涂鸦。
ex_en: Graffiti has been found on ancient Roman walls, including political messages and jokes carved in stone.
ex_zh: 在古罗马的墙壁上发现了涂鸦，包括刻在石头上的政治信息和笑话。

def_en: doing what you are told (obedient child, obedient servant, obedient dog)
def_zh: 听话的（听话的孩子，顺从的仆人，听话的狗）
quote_en: My dog is obedient and comes when I call.
quote_zh: 我的狗很听话，我一叫就过来。
ex_en: In ancient Sparta, boys were trained to be extremely obedient, enduring harsh conditions without complaint.
ex_zh: 在古斯巴达，男孩们被训练得极其顺从，毫无怨言地忍受严酷的条件。

def_en: a device that slows fall from an aircraft (deploy a parachute, parachute jump, emergency parachute)
def_zh: 降落伞（打开降落伞，跳伞，紧急降落伞）
quote_en: The skydiver pulled the parachute cord at 3,000 feet.
quote_zh: 跳伞者在3000英尺处拉开了降落伞绳。
ex_en: Leonardo da Vinci sketched a design for a parachute in 1485, but the first successful jump was not until 1797.
ex_zh: 列奥纳多·达·芬奇在1485年画出了降落伞的设计草图，但第一次成功跳伞直到1797年才实现。

def_en: a small, sweet, red berry (fresh raspberry, raspberry jam, raspberry bush)
def_zh: 树莓（新鲜树莓，树莓酱，树莓丛）
quote_en: We picked raspberries from the garden and ate them with cream.
quote_zh: 我们从花园里摘了树莓，和奶油一起吃。
ex_en: Raspberries have been eaten since prehistoric times – seeds have been found in ancient Roman forts in Britain.
ex_zh: 树莓自史前时代就被食用——在英国的古罗马堡垒中发现了种子。

def_en: in good enough condition to be used (serviceable condition, serviceable vehicle, serviceable tool)
def_zh: 可用的（可用状态，可用的车辆，可用的工具）
quote_en: The old bicycle is not pretty, but it is still serviceable.
quote_zh: 那辆旧自行车不好看，但仍然可用。
ex_en: During World War II, many families kept cars and appliances serviceable by repairing them because new ones were unavailable.
ex_zh: 第二次世界大战期间，许多家庭通过修理使汽车和电器保持可用，因为无法买到新的。

def_en: an imagined perfect society (create a utopia, utopian dream, political utopia)
def_zh: 乌托邦（创造乌托邦，乌托邦之梦，政治乌托邦）
quote_en: The book describes a utopia where everyone is happy and equal.
quote_zh: 这本书描述了一个每个人都快乐和平等的乌托邦。
ex_en: Sir Thomas More coined the word "utopia" in 1516 for his book about an imaginary perfect island society.
ex_zh: 托马斯·莫尔爵士在1516年为他关于一个想象中的完美岛屿社会的书籍创造了“乌托邦”一词。

def_en: a ticket or document that can be exchanged for goods or services (gift voucher, discount voucher, travel voucher)
def_zh: 代金券（礼品券，折扣券，旅行券）
quote_en: She received a voucher for £10 off her next purchase.
quote_zh: 她收到了一张下次购物减10英镑的代金券。
ex_en: During World War II, the British government used ration vouchers to control the amount of food people could buy.
ex_zh: 第二次世界大战期间，英国政府使用配给券来控制人们可以购买的食物数量。

def_en: the period of life between childhood and adulthood (early adolescence, during adolescence, adolescent years)
def_zh: 青春期（青春期早期，在青春期期间，青少年时期）
quote_en: Adolescence can be a challenging time of physical and emotional change.
quote_zh: 青春期可能是身体和情感变化的挑战时期。
ex_en: The concept of adolescence as a distinct stage of life only emerged in the early 20th century, thanks to psychologists like G. Stanley Hall.
ex_zh: 青春期作为一个独特生命阶段的概念直到二十世纪初才出现，这要归功于像G.斯坦利·霍尔这样的心理学家。

def_en: a lack or shortage (vitamin deficiency, iron deficiency, learning deficiency)
def_zh: 缺乏（维生素缺乏，缺铁，学习缺陷）
quote_en: A deficiency of vitamin C can lead to scurvy, a disease sailors once faced.
quote_zh: 缺乏维生素C会导致坏血病——一种水手曾经面临的疾病。
ex_en: In the 18th century, British sailors were given limes to prevent scurvy caused by vitamin deficiency.
ex_zh: 十八世纪，英国水手被给予柠檬来预防由维生素缺乏引起的坏血病。

def_en: containing fibres or stringy parts (fibrous root, fibrous tissue, fibrous vegetable)
def_zh: 纤维状的（须根，纤维组织，纤维质蔬菜）
quote_en: Celery is fibrous, which is why it is good for digestion.
quote_zh: 芹菜富含纤维，这就是为什么它对消化有益。
ex_en: The stem of a palm tree is extremely fibrous, making it flexible enough to withstand strong winds.
ex_zh: 棕榈树的茎非常纤维化，使其足够柔韧以抵御强风。

def_en: a room or building for sports and exercise (school gymnasium, indoor gymnasium, basketball gymnasium)
def_zh: 体育馆（学校体育馆，室内体育馆，篮球馆）
quote_en: The basketball team practised in the gymnasium every afternoon.
quote_zh: 篮球队每天下午在体育馆训练。
ex_en: The word "gymnasium" comes from the ancient Greek "gymnasion," where athletes trained naked (gymnos means naked).
ex_zh: “gymnasium”一词来自古希腊语“gymnasion”，运动员在那里裸体训练（gymnos意为裸体）。

def_en: decorative items worn on the body (gold jewellery, expensive jewellery, jewellery box)
def_zh: 珠宝（黄金珠宝，昂贵的珠宝，珠宝盒）
quote_en: She wore beautiful jewelry on her wedding day.
quote_zh: 她在婚礼那天戴着漂亮的珠宝。
ex_en: The discovery of King Tutankhamun's tomb in 1922 revealed incredible jewelry made of gold and precious stones.
ex_zh: 1922年图坦卡蒙国王墓的发现揭示了用黄金和宝石制成的令人难以置信的珠宝。

def_en: a disease or illness (serious malady, chronic malady, social malady)
def_zh: 疾病（严重疾病，慢性病，社会弊病）
quote_en: The mysterious malady spread through the village, causing fever and chills.
quote_zh: 这种神秘的疾病在村子里传播，引起发烧和发冷。
ex_en: In the 14th century, the Black Death was a deadly malady that killed about one-third of Europe's population.
ex_zh: 十四世纪，黑死病是一种致命的疾病，杀死了欧洲约三分之一的人口。

def_en: loss of ability to move part of the body (muscle paralysis, facial paralysis, sleep paralysis)
def_zh: 瘫痪（肌肉瘫痪，面瘫，睡眠瘫痪）
quote_en: The accident caused temporary paralysis of his left arm.
quote_zh: 事故导致他左臂暂时瘫痪。
ex_en: Franklin D. Roosevelt was struck by polio at age 39, leaving him with permanent leg paralysis, but he became US president anyway.
ex_zh: 富兰克林·德拉诺·罗斯福在39岁时患小儿麻痹症，导致永久性腿部瘫痪，但他仍然成为了美国总统。

def_en: to spread throughout something (permeate the air, permeate society, water permeates)
def_zh: 渗透（弥漫在空气中，渗透到社会，水渗透）
quote_en: The smell of fresh bread permeated the entire house.
quote_zh: 新鲜面包的气味弥漫了整个房子。
ex_en: In the 1960s, new ideas about civil rights began to permeate American society, leading to major legal changes.
ex_zh: 二十世纪六十年代，关于民权的新思想开始渗透到美国社会，导致了重大的法律变革。

def_en: a ghost or imaginary figure (phantom limb, phantom ship, phantom menace)
def_zh: 幽灵（幻肢，幽灵船，幽灵的威胁）
quote_en: The old castle is said to be haunted by a phantom in a black cloak.
quote_zh: 据说那座古老的城堡里有一个穿黑色斗篷的幽灵出没。
ex_en: The "phantom time hypothesis" is a fringe theory that claims the years 614–911 AD never existed – but historians reject it.
ex_zh: “幽灵时间假说”是一个边缘理论，声称公元614–911年从未存在过——但历史学家拒绝接受它。

def_en: ideally or if possible (preferably in writing, preferably before noon, preferably fresh)
def_zh: 最好（最好书面形式，最好中午前，最好新鲜）
quote_en: You should bring a snack, preferably something healthy like fruit.
quote_zh: 你应该带一份零食，最好是水果之类健康的。
ex_en: When packing for a desert hike, bring water, preferably in a metal bottle that stays cool.
ex_zh: 为沙漠徒步打包时，带上水，最好放在能保持凉爽的金属瓶里。

def_en: a set of printed questions for a survey (fill out a questionnaire, online questionnaire, customer questionnaire)
def_zh: 问卷（填写问卷，在线问卷，客户问卷）
quote_en: The doctor asked me to complete a health questionnaire before my appointment.
quote_zh: 医生让我在就诊前填写一份健康问卷。
ex_en: The first modern questionnaire was developed by Sir Francis Galton in the 19th century to study human intelligence.
ex_zh: 第一份现代问卷是由弗朗西斯·高尔顿爵士在十九世纪为研究人类智力而开发的。

def_en: not moving (stationary car, stationary bike, remain stationary)
def_zh: 静止的（静止的汽车，固定自行车，保持静止）
quote_en: The train was stationary at the platform for twenty minutes.
quote_zh: 火车在站台停了二十分钟。
ex_en: In 1851, Léon Foucault proved the Earth rotates by showing that a swinging pendulum appears to change direction while the Earth below is stationary in space? (Actually the pendulum stays fixed, Earth moves.)
ex_zh: 1851年，莱昂·傅科通过展示摆动的摆锤似乎改变方向而证明了地球在自转——而地球在空间中并非静止。

def_en: writing materials like paper, envelopes, and pens (office stationery, personalised stationery, stationery shop)
def_zh: 文具（办公文具，个性化文具，文具店）
quote_en: She bought new stationery for school: notebooks, folders, and pens.
quote_zh: 她为学校买了新文具：笔记本、文件夹和笔。
ex_en: The word "stationery" comes from medieval "stationer" – a bookseller who had a fixed stall (station) in the market.
ex_zh: “stationery”一词来自中世纪的“stationer”——一个在市场上有固定摊位（station）的书商。

def_en: an evil character in a story (movie villain, archvillain, villain of the piece)
def_zh: 恶棍（电影反派，大反派，作品中的坏人）
quote_en: In the story, the villain tries to steal the hero's treasure.
quote_zh: 在故事中，恶棍试图偷走英雄的宝藏。
ex_en: The character of Iago in Shakespeare's *Othello* is considered one of the greatest villains in literature.
ex_zh: 莎士比亚《奥赛罗》中的伊阿古被认为是最伟大的文学反派之一。

def_en: to refuse to give something (withhold information, withhold payment, withhold consent)
def_zh: 扣留（隐瞒信息，拒绝付款，不同意）
quote_en: The company decided to withhold the employee's bonus until the project was finished.
quote_zh: 公司决定在项目完成前扣留员工的奖金。
ex_en: During the Watergate scandal, President Nixon tried to withhold recorded tapes, leading to his resignation in 1974.
ex_zh: 在水门事件期间，尼克松总统试图隐瞒录音带，导致他于1974年辞职。

def_en: a person one knows but not a close friend (casual acquaintance, mutual acquaintance, old acquaintance)
def_zh: 熟人（泛泛之交，共同的熟人，老相识）
quote_en: He is not a close friend, just an acquaintance from summer camp.
quote_zh: 他不是密友，只是夏令营的一个熟人。
ex_en: Benjamin Franklin had many acquaintances in France, which helped him secure French support during the American Revolution.
ex_zh: 本杰明·富兰克林在法国有很多熟人，这帮助他在美国独立战争期间获得了法国的支持。

def_en: an underground room for burial (church crypt, ancient crypt, burial crypt)
def_zh: 地下室（教堂地下室，古代地下室，墓穴）
quote_en: The old church has a crypt where monks were buried centuries ago.
quote_zh: 那座古老的教堂有一个地下室，几个世纪前僧侣们被埋葬在那里。
ex_en: The crypt of St. Peter's Basilica in Rome is believed to contain the tomb of Saint Peter himself.
ex_zh: 罗马圣彼得大教堂的地下室据信包含圣彼得本人的坟墓。

def_en: the front of a building; a false appearance (building facade, grand facade, put up a facade)
def_zh: 正面（建筑正面，宏伟的正面，装样子）
quote_en: The marble facade of the courthouse was impressive.
quote_zh: 法院的大理石正面令人印象深刻。
ex_en: Behind his friendly facade, the spy was gathering secrets for a foreign government.
ex_zh: 在他友好的外表背后，间谍正在为外国政府收集秘密。

def_en: intending to harm or upset (malicious gossip, malicious intent, malicious software)
def_zh: 恶意的（恶意八卦，恶意意图，恶意软件）
quote_en: Spreading false rumours is a malicious act.
quote_zh: 散布虚假谣言是一种恶意行为。
ex_en: In 2017, a malicious cyberattack called WannaCry infected computers worldwide, demanding ransom payments.
ex_zh: 2017年，名为“想哭”的恶意网络攻击感染了全球电脑，要求支付赎金。

def_en: harmful and likely to cause death (malignant tumour, malignant cells, malignant disease)
def_zh: 恶性的（恶性肿瘤，恶性细胞，恶性疾病）
quote_en: The doctor said the tumour was malignant and required immediate treatment.
quote_zh: 医生说肿瘤是恶性的，需要立即治疗。
ex_en: In 1882, German doctor Theodor Klebs first identified the malignant bacteria that cause diphtheria.
ex_zh: 1882年，德国医生特奥多尔·克莱布斯首次发现了引起白喉的恶性细菌。

def_en: suggesting something bad will happen (ominous cloud, ominous sign, ominous silence)
def_zh: 不祥的（不祥的云，不祥之兆，不祥的寂静）
quote_en: The sudden drop in temperature was an ominous sign of the coming storm.
quote_zh: 气温骤降是暴风雨来临的不祥之兆。
ex_en: Before the eruption of Mount Vesuvius in 79 AD, there were ominous tremors that many residents ignored.
ex_zh: 公元79年维苏威火山爆发前，有许多不祥的震动，但许多居民忽略了。

def_en: a very steep cliff; a dangerous situation (edge of a precipice, stand on a precipice, financial precipice)
def_zh: 悬崖（悬崖边缘，站在悬崖边，财政悬崖）
quote_en: The hiker stood at the edge of the precipice, looking down at the valley.
quote_zh: 徒步者站在悬崖边缘，俯瞰山谷。
ex_en: In 1939, Europe stood on the precipice of war as Germany invaded Poland.
ex_zh: 1939年，随着德国入侵波兰，欧洲站在了战争的悬崖边上。

def_en: a person who held a job before someone else (direct predecessor, immediate predecessor, worthy predecessor)
def_zh: 前任（直接前任，前任，值得尊敬的前任）
quote_en: The new principal is very different from her predecessor.
quote_zh: 新校长与她的前任非常不同。
ex_en: George Washington's predecessor as commander of the Continental Army was General Thomas Gage, a British officer.
ex_zh: 乔治·华盛顿担任大陆军司令的前任是英国军官托马斯·盖奇将军。

def_en: respected and admired (prestigious award, prestigious university, prestigious position)
def_zh: 有声望的（著名奖项，名牌大学，显赫职位）
quote_en: Winning the science fair is a prestigious honour at our school.
quote_zh: 在科学展上获奖是我们学校的一项荣誉。
ex_en: The Nobel Prize is one of the most prestigious awards in the world, first given in 1901.
ex_zh: 诺贝尔奖是世界上最有声望的奖项之一，于1901年首次颁发。

def_en: spending a lot of time studying (studious student, studious atmosphere, studious habit)
def_zh: 好学的（好学的学生，学习氛围，好学的习惯）
quote_en: She is a studious girl who always finishes her homework early.
quote_zh: 她是一个好学的女孩，总是早早完成作业。
ex_en: The young Marie Curie was so studious that she would often forget to eat while reading in the library.
ex_zh: 年轻的玛丽·居里非常勤奋，在图书馆读书时常常忘记吃饭。

def_en: excessively or more than necessary (unduly worried, unduly harsh, unduly influenced)
def_zh: 过度地（过度担心，过分严厉，过度影响）
quote_en: Don't be unduly worried about the test – you prepared well.
quote_zh: 不要过分担心考试——你准备得很充分。
ex_en: The judge ruled that the police had acted unduly harshly when arresting the teenager.
ex_zh: 法官裁定警察在逮捕这名青少年时行为过分严厉。

def_en: not needed (unnecessary expense, unnecessary risk, unnecessary detail)
def_zh: 不必要的（不必要的开支，不必要的风险，不必要的细节）
quote_en: Packing three coats for a weekend trip is unnecessary.
quote_zh: 为周末旅行打包三件外套是不必要的。
ex_en: The panic of 1873 was partly caused by unnecessary fear among investors, leading to a six-year depression.
ex_zh: 1873年的恐慌部分是由投资者不必要的恐惧引起的，导致了六年的萧条。

def_en: to waver between different opinions or actions (vacillate between, vacillate on an issue, constantly vacillate)
def_zh: 摇摆不定（在……之间摇摆，在问题上摇摆，不断摇摆）
quote_en: He continued to vacillate between ordering pizza and pasta.
quote_zh: 他继续在点披萨还是意大利面之间摇摆不定。
ex_en: During the 1930s, many European leaders vacillated on how to respond to Hitler's aggression, which some say made war more likely.
ex_zh: 二十世纪三十年代，许多欧洲领导人在如何回应希特勒的侵略上摇摆不定，有人说这使战争更有可能发生。

def_en: lively and spirited (vivacious personality, vivacious girl, vivacious performance)
def_zh: 活泼的（活泼的性格，活泼的女孩，活泼的表演）
quote_en: Her vivacious laugh filled the room with joy.
quote_zh: 她活泼的笑声让房间充满了欢乐。
ex_en: Actress Clara Bow, a star of the 1920s, was known for her vivacious "flapper" persona in silent films.
ex_zh: 二十世纪二十年代的影星克拉拉·鲍以其在无声电影中活泼的“轻佻女郎”形象而闻名。

def_en: open to harm or attack (vulnerable to, vulnerable group, financially vulnerable)
def_zh: 易受伤害的（容易受到，弱势群体，经济脆弱）
quote_en: Baby birds are vulnerable to predators until they learn to fly.
quote_zh: 幼鸟在学会飞行之前容易受到捕食者的攻击。
ex_en: After the attack on Pearl Harbor, the US Pacific fleet was vulnerable, but the Japanese failed to finish it off.
ex_zh: 珍珠港袭击后，美国太平洋舰队很脆弱，但日本人未能将其彻底摧毁。

def_en: something that came before (historical antecedent, direct antecedent, antecedent event)
def_zh: 前事（历史前例，直接前身，先前事件）
quote_en: The steam engine was an antecedent to the modern train.
quote_zh: 蒸汽机是现代火车的前身。
ex_en: The Greek concept of democracy was an antecedent to the Roman Republic, which later inspired America's founders.
ex_zh: 希腊的民主概念是罗马共和国的前身，后者后来启发了美国的开国者。

def_en: a punctuation mark used to show possession or omitted letters (use an apostrophe, possessive apostrophe, missing apostrophe)
def_zh: 撇号（使用撇号，所有格撇号，遗漏撇号）
quote_en: Don't forget the apostrophe in "it's" – it means "it is".
quote_zh: 别忘了“it's”中的撇号——它的意思是“it is”。
ex_en: The apostrophe was introduced into English in the 16th century to show missing letters, like "lov'd" for "loved".
ex_zh: 撇号在十六世纪被引入英语，用于显示缺失的字母，例如用“lov'd”表示“loved”。

def_en: causing difficulty or embarrassment (awkward silence, awkward moment, awkward position)
def_zh: 尴尬的（尴尬的沉默，尴尬的时刻，尴尬的姿势）
quote_en: The conversation became awkward when he mentioned the argument.
quote_zh: 当他提到那次争吵时，谈话变得尴尬起来。
ex_en: In 1972, the Apollo 17 astronauts had an awkward moment when one of them accidentally knocked over a camera on the Moon.
ex_zh: 1972年，阿波罗17号的宇航员有一个尴尬的时刻，其中一人不小心在月球上打翻了一台相机。

def_en: out of control in a violent way (go berserk, run berserk, berserk rage)
def_zh: 狂暴的（发狂，疯狂奔跑，狂暴愤怒）
quote_en: The bull went berserk and chased the farmer out of the field.
quote_zh: 公牛发狂了，把农夫追出了田地。
ex_en: The word "berserk" comes from Old Norse "berserkr," meaning warriors who fought in a wild, trance‑like fury.
ex_zh: “berserk”一词来自古挪威语“berserkr”，意为以狂野、恍惚般的愤怒作战的战士。

def_en: a desk with drawers (writing bureau, oak bureau); a government department (Federal Bureau of Investigation, tourist bureau)
def_zh: 书桌（写字台，橡木书桌）；局（联邦调查局，旅游局）
quote_en: She kept her letters in the top drawer of her bureau.
quote_zh: 她把信放在书桌最上面的抽屉里。
ex_en: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) was founded in 1908 to investigate federal crimes.
ex_zh: 联邦调查局（FBI）成立于1908年，负责调查联邦犯罪。

def_en: a lizard that changes colour (colour‑changing chameleon, panther chameleon, chameleon effect)
def_zh: 变色龙（变色蜥蜴，豹纹变色龙，变色龙效应）
quote_en: The chameleon blended perfectly into the green leaf.
quote_zh: 变色龙完美地融入了绿叶。
ex_en: Chameleons change colour not only for camouflage but also to communicate with other chameleons.
ex_zh: 变色龙变色不仅是为了伪装，也是为了与其他变色龙交流。

def_en: a state of balance (chemical equilibrium, equilibrium point, maintain equilibrium)
def_zh: 平衡（化学平衡，平衡点，保持平衡）
quote_en: The gymnast held her pose with perfect equilibrium.
quote_zh: 体操运动员以完美的平衡保持姿势。
ex_en: Isaac Newton's third law states that for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction, keeping systems in equilibrium.
ex_zh: 艾萨克·牛顿第三定律指出，每一个作用力都有一个大小相等、方向相反的反作用力，使系统保持平衡。

def_en: causing trouble in a playful way (mischievous grin, mischievous child, mischievous behaviour)
def_zh: 淘气的（淘气的笑容，调皮的孩子，恶作剧行为）
quote_en: The mischievous puppy hid my shoes as a game.
quote_zh: 那只淘气的小狗把我的鞋子藏起来当游戏。
ex_en: In Greek mythology, Hermes was a mischievous god who stole Apollo's cattle on the very day he was born.
ex_zh: 在希腊神话中，赫尔墨斯是一个淘气的神，在他出生那天就偷了阿波罗的牛。

def_en: a sentimental longing for the past (feel nostalgia, wave of nostalgia, childhood nostalgia)
def_zh: 怀旧（感到怀旧，怀旧浪潮，童年怀旧）
quote_en: Looking at old photos filled her with nostalgia for summer camp.
quote_zh: 看旧照片让她充满了对夏令营的怀旧之情。
ex_en: The word "nostalgia" was coined in 1688 by a Swiss doctor who thought it was a disease affecting homesick soldiers.
ex_zh: “nostalgia”一词是由一位瑞士医生在1688年创造的，他认为这是一种影响思乡士兵的疾病。

def_en: easily seen or noticed (noticeable difference, noticeable improvement, barely noticeable)
def_zh: 明显的（明显差异，显著改善，几乎察觉不到）
quote_en: There was a noticeable change in his behaviour after the talk.
quote_zh: 谈话后他的行为有了明显变化。
ex_en: In 1859, Charles Darwin noticed that finches on different Galápagos islands had noticeable differences in their beaks.
ex_zh: 1859年，查尔斯·达尔文注意到加拉帕戈斯群岛上不同岛屿的雀类在喙上有明显差异。

def_en: an antibiotic drug derived from mould (penicillin injection, penicillin allergy, discover penicillin)
def_zh: 青霉素（青霉素注射，青霉素过敏，发现青霉素）
quote_en: Penicillin saved many lives during World War II by treating infected wounds.
quote_zh: 青霉素在第二次世界大战期间通过治疗感染伤口挽救了许多生命。
ex_en: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928 when he noticed mould had killed bacteria in a petri dish.
ex_zh: 亚历山大·弗莱明在1928年发现青霉素，当时他注意到培养皿中的霉菌杀死了细菌。

def_en: a prison for serious criminals (state penitentiary, maximum‑security penitentiary, federal penitentiary)
def_zh: 监狱（州立监狱，最高安全级别监狱，联邦监狱）
quote_en: The thief was sent to the state penitentiary for five years.
quote_zh: 那个小偷被送进了州监狱，刑期五年。
ex_en: The Eastern State Penitentiary in Philadelphia, opened in 1829, was the world's first true penitentiary designed to reform prisoners.
ex_zh: 费城的东方州立监狱于1829年开放，是世界上第一所旨在改造囚犯的真正监狱。

def_en: an infection that inflames the lungs (bacterial pneumonia, viral pneumonia, double pneumonia)
def_zh: 肺炎（细菌性肺炎，病毒性肺炎，双肺肺炎）
quote_en: He caught pneumonia after walking in the rain without a coat.
quote_zh: 他在雨中不穿外套行走后患上了肺炎。
ex_en: Before antibiotics, pneumonia was often fatal, killing famous people like George Washington in 1799.
ex_zh: 在抗生素出现之前，肺炎常常是致命的，1799年杀死了乔治·华盛顿等名人。

def_en: a false name used by an author (pen name, under a pseudonym, famous pseudonym)
def_zh: 笔名（笔名，使用笔名，著名笔名）
quote_en: Samuel Clemens wrote under the pseudonym Mark Twain.
quote_zh: 塞缪尔·克莱门斯用笔名马克·吐温写作。
ex_en: Mary Ann Evans wrote under the male pseudonym George Eliot because female authors were not taken seriously in Victorian England.
ex_zh: 玛丽·安·埃文斯用男性笔名乔治·艾略特写作，因为在维多利亚时代的英国女性作家不被重视。

def_en: a split between groups due to disagreement (church schism, political schism, great schism)
def_zh: 分裂（教会分裂，政治分裂，大分裂）
quote_en: The disagreement caused a schism in the club, splitting it into two.
quote_zh: 分歧导致了俱乐部的分裂，一分为二。
ex_en: The Great Schism of 1054 divided Christianity into the Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches.
ex_zh: 1054年的东西方教会大分裂将基督教分为罗马天主教和东正教。
